method_atom: A construction in which the ground-state amplitude of a higher-dimensional many-body/field system is engineered to equal the statistical partition sum of a lower-dimensional system, achieved by tuning the field theory to an anisotropic (Lifshitz-type) quantum critical point with dynamical critical exponent z=2 and coupling scale-invariant critical matter fields to an equally critical dynamical geometry. A second-quantized version of this correspondence recovers the full partition sum when the many-body ground state condenses into a coherent (Bose-Einstein) condensate of the elementary units, correlated across all topological sectors.
object_atom: Quantum membranes (two-dimensional extended objects and their worldvolume), the bosonic-string worldsheet whose partition function they are built to reproduce, and a spacetime realized as a condensate of such membranes.
brief_reason: The transferable part is the Lifshitz z=2 critical-point machinery that makes one system's ground-state wavefunction reproduce another system's partition function via a condensate of elementary units; the membranes, strings, and worldvolume gravity are the string-theory-specific objects.
